content:
FINAL IVX REELS REFACTOR — INSTAGRAM-STYLE ARCHITECTURE, LANDING PAGE SYNC, CRASH ELIMINATION

The IVX Reels module is crashing and must be refactored end-to-end.

Do not patch the existing crash with temporary retries.
Do not add another independent reels implementation.
Do not create duplicate media tables.
Do not claim completion based only on API responses.
Do not use fake Instagram code or claim access to Instagram proprietary source code.

Implement a production-grade Instagram-style reels architecture using our existing Expo React Native, Expo Router, Supabase, backend and landing page.

==================================================
PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
==================================================

Build one unified media and reels system that works across:

1. Native Android app
2. Native iOS app
3. Expo development/testing
4. Public landing page
5. Property detail pages
6. Admin/Owner media management
7. Member feed
8. Reels feed

The same published media record must appear everywhere without duplication.

==================================================
PHASE 1 — CAPTURE THE EXACT CRASH
==================================================

Before changing code, reproduce the crash and capture:

- exact route
- exact reel ID
- exact media URL
- device model
- Android version
- app version
- runtimeVersion
- GitHub SHA
- stack trace
- JavaScript error
- native Android error
- memory usage before crash
- number of mounted video players
- active list index
- previous index
- next index
- playback state
- network status
- media format
- file size
- video dimensions
- codec
- duration

Capture logs using:

adb logcat -c
adb logcat | grep -i -E "AndroidRuntime|FATAL|Expo|ReactNative|video|media|codec|memory|OOM|IOException|IllegalState|ExoPlayer"

Also capture the Metro/device JavaScript console.

Return the exact root cause. Do not return “possibly,” “likely,” or generic narrative.

==================================================
PHASE 2 — AUDIT ALL EXISTING REELS CODE
==================================================

Search the full repository for:

- Video
- expo-video
- expo-av
- AVPlaybackStatus
- FlatList
- FlashList
- RecyclerListView
- reels
- reel
- project_videos
- jv_deal_reels
- media
- videoUrl
- playback
- viewabilityConfig
- onViewableItemsChanged
- useFocusEffect
- AppState
- activeIndex
- currentIndex
- shouldPlay
- isPlaying
- mute
- pause
- unloadAsync
- replaceAsync
- preload
- cache
- thumbnail

Identify and document:

- every reels screen
- every video component
- every media query
- every media table
- every API endpoint
- every landing-page media query
- every property-card video query
- duplicated implementations
- dead code
- placeholder media
- local mock data
- deprecated expo-av usage
- unstable keys
- index-based keys
- nested scrolling containers
- video players mounted outside the visible area
- videos that continue playing after route changes

There must be one canonical reels implementation after refactoring.

==================================================
PHASE 3 — CREATE ONE CANONICAL MEDIA MODEL
==================================================

Use one canonical media record for native app and landing page.

Required canonical shape:

{
  id: string,
  project_id: string | null,
  property_id: string | null,
  deal_id: string | null,
  owner_id: string,
  media_type: "video" | "image",
  source_url: string,
  playback_url: string,
  thumbnail_url: string | null,
  poster_url: string | null,
  width: number | null,
  height: number | null,
  duration_ms: number | null,
  file_size_bytes: number | null,
  mime_type: string | null,
  codec: string | null,
  status: "processing" | "published" | "hidden" | "failed",
  visibility: "public" | "members" | "private",
  caption: string | null,
  display_order: number,
  created_at: string,
  updated_at: string
}

Use the existing real table if it already represents this model.

Do not create a second competing source of truth.

If existing tables include:

- project_videos
- jv_deal_reels
- media
- property_media
- reels

then create a migration plan and one compatibility layer or database view.

All application surfaces must read from the same canonical output.

==================================================
PHASE 4 — UNIFIED API
==================================================

Create or repair these endpoints:

GET /api/reels
GET /api/reels/:id
GET /api/properties/:id/media
GET /api/deals/:id/media
POST /api/reels/:id/view
POST /api/reels/:id/like
DELETE /api/reels/:id/like
GET /api/reels/:id/comments
POST /api/reels/:id/comments
DELETE /api/reels/:id/comments/:commentId
POST /api/reels/:id/share
POST /api/reels/:id/save
DELETE /api/reels/:id/save

Feed endpoint requirements:

GET /api/reels?cursor=<cursor>&limit=10

Response:

{
  items: [],
  nextCursor: string | null,
  hasMore: boolean
}

Requirements:

- cursor pagination
- maximum 10 initial items
- stable ordering
- no duplicate IDs
- published media only
- public visibility for landing page
- authenticated visibility rules for member app
- signed URLs refreshed correctly
- no expired URLs delivered
- no N+1 query pattern
- engagement totals included
- current-user engagement state included when authenticated
- safe anonymous response for landing page

==================================================
PHASE 5 — INSTAGRAM-STYLE PLAYER LIFECYCLE
==================================================

Critical architecture:

Only the active visible reel may play.

Maximum mounted video players:

- previous item
- active item
- next item

Maximum actively playing players:

- exactly 1

All other items must display poster/thumbnail only.

Implement:

- current active reel ID
- active index
- screen focus state
- app foreground/background state
- list visibility state
- player-ready state
- network state

Playback condition:

shouldPlay =
  isScreenFocused &&
  isAppForeground &&
  isItemActive &&
  isAtLeast80PercentVisible

When shouldPlay becomes false:

- pause immediately
- stop audio immediately
- do not continue background playback

When an item moves more than one position away:

- release or unload the player
- retain only metadata and poster image

Never create one persistent player per feed item.

Never autoplay several players simultaneously.

==================================================
PHASE 6 — LIST VIRTUALIZATION
==================================================

Use one vertical virtualized list.

Do not place the reels feed inside:

- ScrollView
- another FlatList
- nested vertical scroll
- animated wrapper that disables recycling

Required list behavior:

- pagingEnabled or equivalent snap behavior
- stable keyExtractor using reel.id
- no index keys
- fixed item height based on visible viewport
- getItemLayout where appropriate
- removeClippedSubviews tested safely on Android
- low initial render count
- small window size
- controlled batch rendering
- no unnecessary rerender of previous reels

Suggested targets:

initialNumToRender: 2
maxToRenderPerBatch: 2
windowSize: 3

Use actual device testing to adjust these values.

Memoize:

- reel item component
- action rail
- caption
- engagement state
- poster component
- player wrapper

Do not rerender every item when one reel receives a like.

==================================================
PHASE 7 — VIDEO COMPONENT REFACTOR
==================================================

Create one reusable component:

components/reels/ReelVideoPlayer.tsx

Responsibilities:

- render poster before player is ready
- initialize player only when near active index
- play only when active
- pause on blur
- pause on background
- recover from transient network failure
- show retry control
- prevent infinite reload loops
- expose playback diagnostics
- clean up all listeners
- release player resources
- handle mute state
- handle replay
- handle buffering
- handle failed source
- handle signed URL expiration

Do not make API calls directly inside the low-level player component.

Use separate hooks:

hooks/useReelsFeed.ts
hooks/useReelPlayback.ts
hooks/useReelEngagement.ts
hooks/useAppForeground.ts
hooks/useNetworkState.ts
hooks/useMediaDiagnostics.ts

==================================================
PHASE 8 — MEMORY AND CRASH PROTECTION
==================================================

Add protection against:

- out-of-memory crashes
- multiple player mounting
- stale player references
- state updates after unmount
- repeated source replacement
- recursive retries
- duplicate event listeners
- infinite viewability callbacks
- corrupted media
- unsupported codec
- unavailable signed URL
- missing poster
- oversized media
- rapid swipe races
- route focus races
- background/foreground races
- Android activity recreation

Add a development diagnostic panel showing:

- mounted players count
- active players count
- active reel ID
- active index
- loaded source
- buffer state
- playback state
- memory warning count
- retry count
- last error
- network type

Production must log this data without exposing private information.

==================================================
PHASE 9 — MEDIA PROCESSING REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

Before a video becomes published, verify or generate:

- mobile-compatible MP4/HLS playback
- H.264 video where supported
- AAC audio where supported
- poster image
- thumbnail
- width
- height
- duration
- file size
- MIME type
- processing status

Reject or quarantine unsupported media.

Do not allow raw unprocessed uploads to enter the public reels feed.

For large files:

- use resumable upload
- show upload progress
- process asynchronously
- publish only after validation succeeds

==================================================
PHASE 10 — LANDING PAGE SYNC
==================================================

The landing page and app must use the same canonical reels API.

Required public landing behavior:

- show 3 property/deal cards, then 1 reel
- repeat the 3-card + 1-reel pattern
- preserve display_order
- use stable media IDs
- render poster before playback
- do not autoplay multiple videos
- pause a reel when it leaves the viewport
- pause when browser tab loses focus
- lazy-load videos
- never download every video during first page load
- no layout shift when poster changes to video
- fixed aspect-ratio container
- no jumping while media loads
- no black empty section
- no duplicate reel
- no duplicate property
- no mobile scroll reset

Ordering requirement:

ORDER BY display_order ASC, created_at DESC

Use one shared normalized response for:

- native Reels
- landing-page Reels
- property detail
- deal detail
- Admin HQ preview

==================================================
PHASE 11 — ENGAGEMENT SYNC
==================================================

Likes, comments, views, shares and saves must synchronize across all platforms.

Required behavior:

- optimistic like update
- rollback if API fails
- idempotent like endpoint
- one view-count session policy
- no duplicate comments
- stable comment IDs
- timestamps from server
- owner moderation
- delete/hide controls
- realtime update or controlled refresh
- anonymous landing visitors may view
- login required for like/comment/save
- return user to the same reel after login

Never store engagement only in component state.

==================================================
PHASE 12 — NAVIGATION AND STATE
==================================================

Required navigation behavior:

- open app directly to a specific reel by ID
- preserve current reel when returning from comments
- preserve mute preference
- restore feed position safely
- do not restore to an invalid index
- do not crash after app restart
- do not auto-open a deleted reel
- handle unavailable reel with a safe fallback
- Android back button works
- route changes stop playback
- owner/admin routes do not mount reels in background

==================================================
PHASE 13 — ERROR UI
==================================================

Never display a full-screen crash for one failed video.

For a failed reel:

- keep the rest of the feed operational
- show poster
- show “Video unavailable”
- provide Retry
- record reel ID and exact error
- allow swipe to the next reel

Use an ErrorBoundary around each reel item and one around the feed route.

One broken media record must not crash the application.

==================================================
PHASE 14 — AMAZON-STYLE REGISTRATION REFERENCE
==================================================

The uploaded Amazon registration image is a visual reference only.

Do not copy Amazon branding, logo, colors, terms or proprietary assets.

Use the interaction concept only:

- Register/Login tabs
- clear input fields
- visible password control
- strong primary button
- simple mobile layout

Adapt it to IVX branding and the existing 3-screen member registration flow.

Registration must remain synchronized with:

- Supabase Auth
- member profile
- Members module
- Admin HQ
- landing-page conversion tracking

Do not mix the registration UI refactor into the reels player lifecycle.

Keep them as separate components and separate QA sections.

==================================================
PHASE 15 — REQUIRED TEST MATRIX
==================================================

Test at minimum:

ANDROID:
- low-memory Android device
- modern Samsung device
- Wi-Fi
- cellular
- airplane mode after cache
- slow connection
- rapid 20-reel swiping
- app background/foreground
- screen lock/unlock
- route leave/return
- three cold starts
- 10-minute continuous playback
- 30-minute feed session

IOS:
- iPhone Safari landing page
- native app
- mute switch behavior
- background/foreground
- rapid swipe

WEB:
- Android Chrome
- desktop Chrome
- Safari
- tablet viewport
- mobile viewport
- browser tab hidden/restored

FUNCTIONAL:
- play
- pause
- replay
- mute
- unmute
- next
- previous
- like
- unlike
- comment
- delete comment
- share
- save
- unsave
- open property
- open deal
- return to same reel

DATA:
- no duplicate IDs
- no expired URLs
- no missing posters
- no unpublished media
- same reel totals across native and landing
- same caption across native and landing
- same property/deal relationship everywhere

==================================================
PHASE 16 — PERFORMANCE ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
==================================================

The refactor fails unless all are true:

- active video players: exactly 1
- mounted video players: maximum 3
- no crash after 50 consecutive swipes
- no crash after 30-minute session
- no duplicate playback audio
- no video continues after leaving route
- no black screen during normal loading
- no page jump when poster/video loads
- no full feed rerender after one like
- first reel poster appears immediately
- first video begins after ready state
- next reel is prepared without mounting the full feed
- memory returns toward baseline after leaving reels
- one failed video does not crash the feed
- landing page does not preload all reels
- landing page and native app return identical canonical media IDs

==================================================
PHASE 17 — DEPLOYMENT
==================================================

After implementation:

1. Run TypeScript checks.
2. Run lint.
3. Run unit tests.
4. Run API tests.
5. Run database migration verification.
6. Run Expo Doctor.
7. Export Android bundle.
8. Create a clean Android test build.
9. Deploy backend.
10. Deploy landing page.
11. Verify domain and CDN cache.
12. Install fresh Android build.
13. Test on the owner Android device.
14. Capture logs during the full test.
15. Confirm the deployed SHAs match.

Do not use an old OTA update to test a native dependency change.

If video/native packages changed, create a fresh APK/build.

==================================================
PHASE 18 — REQUIRED EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return one final evidence table:

ITEM | EXPECTED | ACTUAL | PASS/FAIL | EVIDENCE

Include:

- root cause
- crash stack trace
- fixed file paths
- database migration
- canonical media table/view
- reels API URL
- landing-page API URL
- GitHub commit SHA
- frontend deployment ID
- backend deployment ID
- Android build ID
- APK URL
- runtimeVersion
- Expo project ID
- active player count
- mounted player count
- memory before session
- memory after 30-minute session
- 50-swipe result
- landing-page sync result
- engagement sync result
- member registration sync result
- Android screen recording
- landing-page screen recording
- console logs
- adb logs
- zero-critical-error proof

==================================================
FINAL STATUS RULE
==================================================

Do not report COMPLETE, FIXED, VERIFIED, CERTIFIED or PRODUCTION READY until:

1. The owner Android device completes 50 reel swipes without a crash.
2. Only one reel plays at a time.
3. The feed survives a 30-minute session.
4. The same reel records appear on the native app and landing page.
5. Likes/comments/views remain synchronized.
6. The landing page does not jump or show black media sections.
7. A fresh APK opens successfully.
8. Deployment SHAs match GitHub.
9. Real video evidence is attached.
10. adb logcat contains no fatal reels/player exception.

CURRENT STATUS:

REELS MODULE = FAILED
LANDING SYNC = UNVERIFIED
DEVICE QA = FAILED
REFACTOR REQUIRED = YES
FINAL TASK = OPEN
